message:
feat(mvcc): add DroVolatilityTracker for sliding-window DRO radius estimation

Introduce a stateful sliding-window tracker that accumulates recent
workload abort-rate and edge-rate observations and produces
Wasserstein-radius certificates on demand. This replaces ad-hoc
one-shot calls to `dro_wasserstein_radius` with a reusable abstraction
that naturally captures workload volatility over time.

New public types in fsqlite-mvcc:
- DroWindowObservation: a single (abort_rate, edge_rate) sample.
- DroObservedRateKind: discriminant for Abort vs Edge rate, with
  Display impl for diagnostics.
- DroVolatilityTrackerError: validation errors for non-finite or
  out-of-range rate values, with Display and std::error::Error impls.
- DroVolatilityTrackerConfig: configurable window_size (default 32)
  and min_samples (default 4), with clamping normalization.
- DroVolatilityTracker: the core ring-buffer tracker exposing
  observe_window(), abort_rate_variance(), edge_rate_variance(),
  radius_certificate(), is_ready(), and sample_count().

Internal helper `validate_observed_rate` rejects NaN/Inf and values
outside [0.0, 1.0] before any sample is recorded, ensuring the
sliding window never contains garbage data.

Four comprehensive tests cover:
- min_samples gating (no certificate before threshold)
- bounded-window eviction (oldest samples drop, latest survive)
- regime-shift detection (volatile workloads produce larger radii)
- invalid-rate rejection (NaN, out-of-range, no side-effects)

The default T3 DRO loss matrix in ssi_validation.rs is migrated from
raw `dro_wasserstein_radius` calls to `DroVolatilityTracker`, feeding
the same four historical (abort, edge) windows through the tracker
API. The resulting certificate is identical, but the code now
exercises the same path that runtime workload monitoring will use.

Re-exports in lib.rs are updated to expose all new types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
